title: Regulation Reduction Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a p
plain_summary: This bill says that before government agencies can make any new rule, they must first get rid of three old rules. If the new rule would cost a lot of money, it cannot cost more than the three old rules that were removed. Agencies also have to tell Congress within 90 days which of their current rules cost too much money or do not work well. The goal is to reduce the total number of government rules by making agencies remove more rules than they add.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for considerati

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Regulation Reduction Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2. Repeal of Regulations Required Before Issuance of a New Rule
sections.text: Before a government agency can create any new rule, it must first get rid of three or more old rules that are related to the new rule if possible. For major rules that cost a lot of money, agencies must also make sure the new rule costs the same amount or less than the three old rules they are removing. A major rule is one that has a very large economic impact. The Office of Management and Budget must check and confirm that the costs work out correctly for major rules. Any old rule that gets removed must be announced in the Federal Register, which is the official government publication for new rules and changes. This law applies to rules that create costs or responsibilities for private people, businesses, or state and local governments. It does not apply to rules about how agencies run themselves internally or buy things, or rules that are being changed to make them less burdensome. Within ninety days after this law passes, each agency head must send a report to Congress and the Office of Management and Budget. The report must review all the agency's current rules and identify which ones are costly, do not work well, repeat what other rules already do, or are out of date. The report must also list any other unnecessary rules that have these same problems. In this law, agency means what it means in section five hundred fifty one of title five of the United States Code. Major rule means what it means in section eight hundred four of title five of the United States Code. Rule means what it means in section five hundred fifty one of title five of the United States Code. State means any of the fifty states, the District of Columbia, any territory or possession of the United States, and any federally recognized Indian Tribe.
